food_subgroup: Fried chicken wings combo meal with sides and beverages

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken wing food product class. No specific entry for a multi-component fried chicken wings combo meal. Closest parent match used; the broader meal context (fries, dips, drinks) is not captured by this URI.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers cooked chicken meat as the dominant component. No composite meal code exists for a fried chicken wings + fries + dip + drink bundle. Closest parent anchor used for the primary protein component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No defensible SNOMED CT code identified for a commercial fried chicken wings combo meal. Left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.description: Full Meals for 2 set: 14 classic wings (2 flavors), 3 regular fries, 2 ranch dips, 2 drinks
serving.natural_language_portion: One full Meals for 2 Classic set as served at Wingstop Singapore, comprising 14 classic fried chicken wings across two flavors, three regular portions of seasoned fries, two ranch dipping sauces, and two standard soft drinks, estimated at approximately 1,400 g total weight for the solid and semi-solid components.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken wing, coated, baked or fried, prepared; French fries, fast food; Ranch dressing; Carbonated beverage, cola
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry exists for the complete Wingstop Meals for 2 Classic bundle. Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated by weighted-average composition of the four component categories: (1) deep-fried breaded chicken wings (dominant component, ~55% by weight), (2) fast-food French fries (~30% by weight), (3) ranch dressing (~5% by weight), and (4) cola-type soft drink (~10% by weight). USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries used for each component. Weighted average computed and expressed per 100 g of the combined solid/semi-solid meal. Drink weight excluded from per-100g calculation to avoid dilution artifact; drink contribution noted separately. Values are estimates with medium confidence.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; fried chicken and French fries analogous entries; University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Wingstop Meals for 2 Classic bundle. GI estimated as a weighted average of the glycemic components: French fries (GI ~63, University of Sydney GI Database), breaded fried chicken (GI ~40, low due to protein and fat blunting), and cola soft drink (GI ~63, University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted by carbohydrate contribution: fries ~60% of total carbohydrate, chicken breading ~25%, cola ~15%. Weighted GI ≈ 65, classified as medium. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 80 g carbohydrate in full meal) / 100 ≈ 52. Serving carbohydrate estimated from component breakdown for the full 1,400 g meal.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; estimated ~1,680 mg for the full meal set excluding drinks)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal, as the combined sodium from seasoned fried chicken wings, salted fries, and ranch dipping sauce can contribute approximately 70–85% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg in a single sitting.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~52 per full meal), high refined carbohydrate from fries and breading, and sugar from cola drink
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal due to its high glycemic load driven by deep-fried breaded chicken, starchy fries, and sugar-sweetened beverages, all of which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; substituting the cola with water and limiting fries portion size would substantially reduce glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and cholesterol from deep-frying and ranch dressing
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This meal is high in saturated fat (~3.2 g per 100 g), contains measurable trans fat (~0.2 g per 100 g from frying oils), and delivers substantial dietary cholesterol (~62 mg per 100 g), making it unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease; occasional consumption should be paired with low-fat, low-sodium meals throughout the day.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wingstop Meals for 2 Classic is a commercial fast-food combo meal sold at Wingstop restaurants in Singapore, available on field survey from outlets including City Square Mall and Funan. The set comprises 14 classic deep-fried chicken wings prepared in two flavors of the customer's choice from Wingstop's classic range (such as Original Hot, Lemon Pepper, Garlic Parmesan, or Hickory Smoked BBQ), three regular portions of seasoned French fries, two ranch dipping sauces, and two standard soft drinks. The chicken wings are coated in a seasoned flour breading and deep-fried in vegetable oil until golden and crispy, yielding a crunchy exterior with juicy, tender meat inside. The fries are thin-cut, salted, and fried to a crisp. Ranch dip adds a creamy, tangy, herb-flavored counterpoint. The meal is certified Halal and is designed for sharing between two people. Nutritionally, this is a calorie-dense, high-fat, high-sodium meal: per 100 g of the combined solid components, it provides approximately 265 kcal, 14.2 g protein, 14.8 g fat (of which 3.2 g saturated), and 20.5 g carbohydrate, with sodium at approximately 480 mg per 100 g. The full meal set is estimated to deliver around 3,700 kcal, 1,680 mg sodium, and a glycemic load of approximately 52 for two people combined. Clinically, this meal poses high concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to its sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and high glycemic load. It is not suitable for regular consumption as part of a health-conscious diet. The meal is priced at SGD 33.40 on field survey data.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for the complete Wingstop Meals for 2 Classic bundle. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted-average composition of USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries for the four component categories (fried breaded chicken wings, fast-food French fries, ranch dressing, cola). GI is an analogous estimate weighted by carbohydrate contribution from University of Sydney GI Database entries for the closest matching foods. Medium confidence reflects the composite estimation approach and the absence of a direct laboratory analysis of this specific commercial product.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100 g of the combined solid and semi-solid meal components (wings, fries, ranch dip). The cola soft drink is excluded from the per-100g base to avoid dilution of the nutrient profile; its contribution is noted in the serving description and glycemic load calculation. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reliably reported for fried chicken or fast-food fries in USDA FoodData Central and is not a standard reported nutrient for this food category. 3. Trans fat (~0.2 g per 100 g) is estimated from residual trans fats in commercial frying oils; actual values may vary by outlet and oil batch. 4. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and all micronutrients are estimated from USDA component data and carry medium confidence. 5. The halal certification is based on Wingstop Singapore's publicly stated halal status (MUIS-certified); this should be verified at time of consumption as certification status may change. 6. Serving size of 1,400 g is an estimate for the solid components of the full set for two people; actual weight will vary by outlet and portion. 7. Glycemic load per serving of 52 is calculated for the full meal set (two persons) using estimated total carbohydrate of ~80 g from fries, breading, and cola combined. 8. All -1 values in per_100g represent nutrients not reported or not reliably estimable from the available USDA component data for this composite meal.
